untranslatable_reason: Signal requires market microstructure inputs (order submission costs in bps, bid-ask spread thresholds, counterparty historical trade data, notional block size) that are NOT available in the allowed technical indicator set. The core logic involves soliciting latent demand based on order costs, spread width, historical counterparty behavior, and block size—none of which can be expressed using only price, volume, and technical indicators. Substituting bb_bandwidth as a proxy for spread width is approximate and loses the semantic intent of the rule.
translation_notes: This signal is fundamentally based on order flow analysis and market microstructure conditions (order costs, counterparty intent, notional sizing) rather than technical price action. The allowed indicators cannot capture: (1) absolute bid-ask spread levels in bps, (2) transaction costs relative to notional size, (3) counterparty trade history, or (4) whether an order has been explicitly declined. A proper translation would require market data feeds (quotes, order book state, trade history) and fundamental trade analytics not present in the technical indicator library.
